blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: What do you think Marcus said to him.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: I don't know. Marcus was — he was capable of things most people aren't. He could walk into a room with someone dangerous and make them feel like they were the ones at a disadvantage. He had that quality.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: I think he was trying to buy me time or make some kind of arrangement. That would be exactly like him — going directly to the problem without telling me so I couldn't talk him out of it.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: And you believe Price might have had Marcus killed because of that call.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: Is that what you think happened?

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: Price is capable of it. I'm not going to pretend otherwise. But having someone killed in a locked bedroom in a beach house while five people are downstairs — that's not how men like Price operate. They're blunt. They're not subtle.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: I've had eight months to think about how men like Price operate.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Mr. Mercer. Where were you between nine forty-five and ten thirty Saturday evening.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: At the dinner table. With everyone else.
His eyes didn't move. Davenport's didn't either.
